title: Bugsnag Error Event
description: Represents an individual error event reported to or retrieved from Bugsnag, containing exception details, stack traces, device and application information, breadcrumbs, and custom metadata.

exceptions.description: The list of exceptions that occurred. The first exception is the most significant. Multiple exceptions represent a causal chain.

breadcrumbs.description: A trail of events leading up to the error, providing context about user actions and application state.

threads.description: Information about all running threads at the time of the error.

context.description: A string representing what was happening in the application at the time of the error, such as a controller action or route name.

groupingHash.description: A custom grouping hash to override default error grouping. Events with the same hash are grouped together.

unhandled.description: Whether this error was unhandled (true) or caught and handled (false) by the application.

severity.description: The severity level of the error.

metaData.description: Custom metadata organized by tab name. Each key represents a tab on the Bugsnag dashboard containing key-value debugging information.

Exception.description: Represents an exception including the error class, message, and stack trace.

errorClass.description: The class or type name of the exception (e.g., TypeError, NullPointerException).

message.description: The human-readable error message describing what went wrong.

stacktrace.description: The stack trace frames, ordered from the error location to the entry point.

StackFrame.description: A single frame in an exception's stack trace.

file.description: The file path where the code is located.

lineNumber.description: The line number in the file.

columnNumber.description: The column number in the file.

inProject.description: Whether this frame originates from the project's own code.

code.description: A map of line numbers to source code lines surrounding the error location.

Breadcrumb.description: A breadcrumb event that occurred before the error.

timestamp.description: The ISO 8601 timestamp when the breadcrumb was recorded.

name.description: A short summary of the breadcrumb event.

metaData.description: Additional metadata associated with the breadcrumb.

Request.description: Information about the HTTP request during which the error occurred.

clientIp.description: The IP address of the client making the request.

httpMethod.description: The HTTP method used (GET, POST, etc.).

Thread.description: Information about a thread running at the time of the error.

errorReportingThread.description: Whether this is the thread on which the error occurred.

stacktrace.description: The stack trace of this thread.

SeverityReason.description: Information about why the severity was set to its current value.

type.description: The reason type for the severity assignment.

attributes.description: Additional attributes about the severity reason.

User.description: Information about the user who experienced the error.

id.description: A unique identifier for the user.

App.description: Information about the application where the error occurred.

releaseStage.description: The release stage (e.g., production, staging, development).

dsymUUIDs.description: UUIDs for dSYM files used in symbolication.

duration.description: Time in milliseconds since the application started.

durationInForeground.description: Time in milliseconds the app has been in the foreground.

inForeground.description: Whether the app was in the foreground when the error occurred.

Device.description: Information about the device on which the error occurred.

browserName.description: The browser name, for web applications.

jailbroken.description: Whether the device is jailbroken or rooted.

time.description: The device's local time when the error occurred.

runtimeVersions.description: Runtime version information (e.g., node, clr, swift).

Session.description: Session information for the current user session.

startedAt.description: The date and time the session started.

handled.description: The number of handled events in this session.

unhandled.description: The number of unhandled events in this session.
